title: taraxacum ostrinum
intro: Taraxacum ostrinum, also known as the lesser dandelion or wild dandelion, is a perennial plant species within the dandelion genus, characterized by its smaller size and less showy flowers compared to its more well-known relative, Taraxacum officinale. The medicinal properties of Taraxacum ostrinum include its high content of vitamins A and C, as well as its rich source of minerals such as potassium, calcium, and iron, which contribute to its health benefits. The health benefits of Taraxacum ostrinum include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health conditions. Taraxacum ostrinum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote overall well-being and vitality.
